metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (with oracle) that explicitly verifies navigation between history rounds, e.g.: 'With ≥2 retained plan-review sidecars, the audit page exposes prev/next controls and the e2e test confirms that navigating to round N-1 renders that round's findings distinct from round N.'
findings.finding: The named success criterion 'navigable across the retained plan- and code-review history' has no corresponding acceptance criterion or oracle test, so an executor could ship a single-round, non-navigable page and pass all ACs.
findings.location: Success Criteria bullet 1 vs Acceptance Criteria bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a third AC under 'Acceptance Criteria': '[ ] With at least two retained plan-review sidecars for a ticket, the audit page renders round-navigation controls and the end-to-end test confirms that selecting a prior round loads that round's findings (verifiable by a field value present in round N-1 but absent in round N).'

Alternatively, extend AC 2's oracle description: '…asserts on the rendered response (including that navigating to a prior review round loads that round's distinct findings).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback/degradation specification: for each UI region, define what the server renders when the Epic 1 source is absent or is a pre-v2 schema (e.g., 'show a banner: "Review predates enrichment — re-run rebar review-plan to generate audit data"').
findings.finding: The plan states 'this epic MUST NOT proceed to render a region until its Epic 1 field source has landed', but no fallback rendering behavior is specified for the intermediate state where a sidecar exists but is schema `plan_review_result_v1` (missing the v2 fields `block_threshold`, `blocking_enabled`, `evidence`, `scenarios`). An auditor who runs the UI before Epic 1's v2 enrichment is deployed will get either an error, empty panels, or silently missing data — and the plan provides no specified degradation protocol.
findings.location: Data contract section: 'This epic MUST NOT proceed to render a region until its Epic 1 field source has landed'

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit depends_on Epic 1 story C to child ff6f, and require the E2E completion-panel oracle to use a fixture that matches the real story C sidecar schema (not an ad-hoc shape), with a note that the test is blocked until story C has landed.
findings.finding: Child ff6f's completion-panel oracle requires story C's criteria[] field (from Epic 1), but neither child declares an explicit depends_on or readiness gate for Epic 1 story C. If Epic 1 story C has not landed when ff6f's E2E tests run, the completion panel's criteria[] will be empty or absent and the oracle will fail silently rather than blocking the story.
findings.location: child ff6f-b655-4058-4308 (Acceptance Criteria — completion panel oracle; Scope — completion panel reads story C criteria[])
findings.suggested_fix: In ff6f's AC for the completion panel, add: 'Oracle prerequisite: Epic 1 story C must be closed and its criteria[] sidecar format finalized before this oracle is run against production data. The E2E fixture must use the canonical story C output schema.' Also add depends_on: Epic 1 story C to ff6f's metadata.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a Decomposition or Stories section that names and briefly scopes the two child stories so AC1 ('Both child stories are closed') maps to described, verifiable work.
findings.finding: AC1 ('Both child stories are closed') references 'child stories' that are never defined, named, or described anywhere in the plan body. There is no decomposition section, no story list, and no description of what the two stories cover, making this criterion unmappable to any described work and unverifiable from the plan alone.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'Both child stories are closed.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Stories' section with two entries, e.g.: 'Story 1 — Server setup & subcommand: implements `rebar audit serve`, the FastAPI/Jinja2 server, loopback binding, config flag, and `[ui]` extra.' 'Story 2 — Audit page rendering: implements the per-ticket audit page with ticket details, plan/completion/code panels, pass-1..4 breakdown, per-finding thresholds, and completion criteria[] rows, plus the end-to-end test.' This anchors AC1 to concrete described work.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a durable documentation artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or design doc) capturing the optional-extra / lazy-import architectural decision and its 'server-rendered over static export' rationale, referenced from the plan.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — an optional FastAPI+Jinja2 web server delivered as a lazy-imported `[ui]` extra with a named data-contract interface — but commits to no durable documentation artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md entry, or design-doc update) capturing this decision and its rationale.
findings.location: Approach rationale section; Compatibility & rollback section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a story task (or AC) to write a short ADR (e.g. `docs/adr/0NNN-optional-ui-extra.md`) covering: (1) decision to use FastAPI+Jinja2 reusing the reviewbot extra, (2) lazy-import invariant ('import rebar must not pull web deps'), (3) static-export alternative considered and rejected, (4) the named Epic 1 data-contract interface. Reference the ADR from CLAUDE.md so it is discoverable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider restructuring child 1 as a thin vertical slice that boots the server AND renders at minimum the ticket-details panel end-to-end (so it is independently testable by an auditor), with child 2 adding the full plan/completion/code panels and history navigation on top.
findings.finding: The two child stories split the epic horizontally by layer (infrastructure scaffolding vs. content rendering) rather than by a thin vertical slice first. Child 1 (web server scaffolding: subcommand, extra, config flag, loopback) delivers no user-visible rendered output on its own, making it not independently valuable to an auditor. A stronger decomposition would have child 1 deliver a thin end-to-end slice (server boots AND renders minimal ticket details) so it is testable and demonstrable in isolation, with child 2 enriching it with the full panel/history/threshold detail.
findings.location: Decomposition state — children cultivated-aquatic-crow and depressant-amethyst-wasp
findings.suggested_fix: Redefine child 1 (crow) to include: server scaffolding + a single working ticket-details render (enough to prove the FastAPI+Jinja2 stack end-to-end). Redefine child 2 (wasp) as: layering the finding-centric IA, gate strip, threshold meter, and history navigation onto the already-working thin page.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the data contract's `scenarios` and `evidence` requirements with the existing sidecar `_slim()` deliberate-exclusion policy: either (a) specify that Epic 1 story A must add `scenarios` and `evidence` to `_slim()` (overriding the lean-sidecar rationale, noting the storage cost), or (b) remove `scenarios`/`evidence` from the required-fields table and document that those fields are sourced from a different path (e.g., live re-read of the verdict rather than the sidecar).
findings.finding: The data contract table lists `scenarios` and `evidence` as required fields for the Finding-provenance UI region, but the existing sidecar `_slim()` function deliberately excludes both (comment: 'deliberately exclude runtime-only carriers (e.g. scenarios, evidence, _agentic)'). If Epic 1 story A's enrichment only adds `block_threshold` and `blocking_enabled` to `_slim()` without also restoring `scenarios` and `evidence`, the UI would silently render empty fields for those two columns — defeating the 'lossless and readable' audit goal for findings that rely on scenario/evidence context.
findings.location: Data contract table ('Finding-provenance' row, required fields: `scenarios`, `evidence`) vs. sidecar.py `_slim()` deliberate exclusion
findings.suggested_fix: In the data contract table, add a footnote clarifying that `scenarios` and `evidence` must be explicitly added to `_slim()` by Epic 1 story A (overriding the current exclusion), OR move them to a separate row noting they are sourced from a live verdict read rather than the persisted sidecar — so the implementer of story A does not accidentally omit them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the approach rationale or the Compatibility section that the new `nava-rebar[ui]` extra must explicitly declare `jinja2` (or `fastapi[all]`) as a dependency — it is NOT currently bundled in the `reviewbot` extra — and update the rationale from 'reusing reviewbot's already-shipped web stack' to 'reusing reviewbot's framework choice (FastAPI + uvicorn)'.
findings.finding: The plan states it 'REUSES the `reviewbot` extra's already-shipped web stack' as part of its approach rationale, but the `reviewbot` extra does not include Jinja2 (`reviewbot = ["fastapi", "uvicorn[standard]", "boto3"]`). Jinja2 is an optional FastAPI dependency. The plan correctly names a new `nava-rebar[ui]` extra, but the rationale conflates 'reusing reviewbot's framework choice' with 'reusing reviewbot's declared extra' — the [ui] extra will still need an explicit `jinja2` (or `fastapi[all]`) dependency declaration that has no precedent in the repo.
findings.location: Approach rationale section: 'REUSING the `reviewbot` extra's already-shipped web stack'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"jinja2>=3"` (or `"fastapi[all]"`) to the planned `[ui]` extra definition and update the rationale sentence to: 'REUSING the `reviewbot` extra's framework choice (FastAPI + uvicorn) rather than adding a second web framework; Jinja2 is added to the new [ui] extra as its server-rendering layer.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'reusing the already-shipped web stack' framing: Jinja2 is absent from the existing `reviewbot` extra (`pyproject.toml:93`); explicitly declare `jinja2` (and `python-multipart` if needed) as a new dependency in the planned `[ui]` extra, and confirm `fastapi.templating.Jinja2Templates` is the intended API.
findings.finding: The plan asserts it is 'REUSING the `reviewbot` extra's already-shipped web stack' for Jinja2 server-side rendering, but Jinja2 is not present in the existing `reviewbot` extra (`pyproject.toml:93` = `["fastapi", "uvicorn[standard]", "boto3"]`). FastAPI's `Jinja2Templates` requires `jinja2` as a separate install — it is not bundled with FastAPI. Jinja2 has zero call-sites anywhere in the codebase, so the plan's 'reuse' framing is incorrect and the Jinja2 capability assertion is unverified.
findings.location: Approach rationale section — 'Server-rendered FastAPI + Jinja2, REUSING the `reviewbot` extra's already-shipped web stack'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach rationale and Scope sections, replace 'REUSING the `reviewbot` extra's already-shipped web stack' with 'sharing the FastAPI + uvicorn pattern established by the reviewbot extra, adding `jinja2` as a net-new dependency to the new `[ui]` extra.' Then in the child story (or the epic's dependency list), explicitly add `jinja2>=3` to the `nava-rebar[ui]` extra definition in `pyproject.toml`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly declare `jinja2` (and any other net-new deps such as `aiofiles` if async templates are needed) in the `nava-rebar[ui]` extra's dependency list, and remove the claim that Jinja2 is part of the existing `reviewbot` web stack — it is not.
findings.finding: The plan asserts it is 'REUSING the reviewbot extra's already-shipped web stack' for Jinja2 server-side rendering, but the reviewbot extra contains no Jinja2 (`pyproject.toml:93` = `["fastapi", "uvicorn[standard]", "boto3"]`). Jinja2 is a net-new dependency absent from every declared dep group, and `fastapi.templating.Jinja2Templates` requires `jinja2` to be separately installed (it is not bundled with FastAPI). The new `nava-rebar[ui]` extra's composition is never specified, silently requiring authors to infer that jinja2 must be added.
findings.location: Approach rationale section: 'Server-rendered FastAPI + Jinja2, REUSING the reviewbot extra's already-shipped web stack'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach rationale, change to: 'Server-rendered FastAPI + Jinja2 (Jinja2 is a net-new dep added to the new `nava-rebar[ui]` extra alongside fastapi and uvicorn[standard]).' In the Scope / Compatibility section, list the exact `[ui]` extra composition: `ui = ["fastapi", "uvicorn[standard]", "jinja2"]`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define, in the data contract table, exactly what v2 sidecar fields Epic 1 must add to support the four-pass breakdown UI (e.g., a `passes` or `pass1_findings[]` / `pass2_verifications[]` sub-object), distinguishing raw Pass-1 findings from the final post-Pass-3 decided list.
findings.finding: The Success Criteria and AC require the UI to show 'each review's four passes shown including pass-1 findings, pass-2 verification, pass-3 determination, pass-4 coaching' as SEPARATE UI regions, but the data contract table only specifies a single enriched per-finding list (the post-Pass-3 decided findings with merged `verification`/`decision` attributes). The raw Pass-1 finding list (pre-verification) and the raw Pass-2 sub-answer outputs are NOT retained in the current sidecar (`_slim()` explicitly excludes `scenarios` and `evidence` as 'runtime-only carriers'), and the plan does not specify a named v2 storage schema that Epic 1 must produce to hold intermediate pass artifacts separately from the final decided set.
findings.location: Success Criteria bullet 1 ('each review's four passes shown including pass-1 findings, pass-2 verification, pass-3 determination'); Data contract table row 'Finding-provenance (per finding)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Four-pass breakdown' row to the data contract table specifying the v2 sidecar fields: e.g., `pass1_findings[]` (raw pre-verification finder output), `pass2_verifications[]` (per-finding verification sub-answers), and clarify that the existing `findings[]` (decided) + `coaching[]` cover passes 3 and 4. Alternatively, explicitly state that 'Pass-1 findings' in the UI means the decided findings whose `decision != dropped` (i.e., no separate pre-verification list is needed) and update the AC language accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a named v2 sidecar schema (e.g., `plan_review_result_v2.schema.json`) that formally adds `block_threshold` and `blocking_enabled` as required per-finding fields, so Epic 1 story A has a machine-verifiable contract to implement against.
findings.finding: `block_threshold` and `blocking_enabled` are listed as Required fields in the data contract table (Finding-provenance region and Threshold meter region), but the current plan-review sidecar `_slim()` function does NOT persist either field. The plan delegates adding these to Epic 1 story A/B ('plan-review v2 `_slim`'), but no v2 schema contract or named schema artifact is defined anywhere in the plan — only an informal field list in the data contract table. Epic 1 has no machine-readable specification of what the v2 sidecar must look like.
findings.location: Data contract table, rows 'Finding-provenance (per finding)' and 'Threshold meter (per finding)'; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py _slim()

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify or create the story that owns the audit read-layer module (story E); add it to this epic's sibling roster and declare it as an explicit depends_on in both a3d7 and ff6f, OR fold the read-layer implementation into one of the two existing children's Scope sections.
findings.finding: Both children (a3d7 and ff6f) depend on a 'story E read layer' — a Python module that reads sidecar/audit data and exposes structured objects to the FastAPI routes — but story E appears in neither child's scope and is absent from the complete 2-child sibling roster. This is an owned-by-none gap: the read layer is required by both routes (index and per-ticket) but no sibling delivers it.
findings.location: child a3d7-fadb-e7e9-4e2c (Scope / index route); child ff6f-b655-4058-4308 (Scope / /ticket/<id> route)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a third child story (story E) to this epic whose scope is the audit read-layer module — a thin Python module (e.g., src/rebar/audit/read_layer.py) that reads the enriched sidecar files produced by Epic 1 and returns typed dataclasses consumed by the server routes. Mark both a3d7 and ff6f as depends_on story E. Alternatively, if the read layer is small enough, fold it into a3d7's Scope (alongside server.py) so the vertical slice is truly end-to-end from the first story.

findings.finding: The plan promises 'each review's four passes shown including thresholds' (Success Criteria) and 'pass-1..4 detail and thresholds' (AC) but never defines what data vocabulary backs the four-pass breakdown display. The existing `plan_review_result_v1` sidecar (`build_payload` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py`) stores only post-aggregated findings (with `decision`/`severity`/`priority`/`verification` per finding) — it does NOT store raw pass-1 pre-filter output, pass-2 per-finding binary sub-answers verbatim, the `block_threshold` value used at pass-3, or full pass-4 coaching move templates. The plan defers to the hard-dependency enrichment epic for 'lossless' data but never specifies which enriched fields that epic must expose to enable the four-pass breakdown the UI must render.
findings.location: Success Criteria; Acceptance Criteria (AC line: 'pass-1..4 detail and thresholds'); Scope ('per-ticket audit page rendering')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Data contract' subsection under Approach (or as an interface table) listing the minimal field set the dependency epic must emit for each of the four panels — e.g. `pass1_findings[]`, `pass2_verifications{}`, `pass3_threshold`, `pass4_coaching[]` — even if the exact names are pending the enrichment epic's design, noting them as TBD-but-required so both stories share a named interface.

findings.finding: Parent AC-3's oracle ('all existing commands behave unchanged') is scope-narrowed by child a3d7: the child only oracle-tests `import rebar` (no web dep) and the `rebar audit serve` disabled path — no child oracle-tests that commands OTHER than `audit serve` (e.g., `rebar review`, `rebar verify`) continue to behave unchanged after the new config key and CLI subcommand are added.
findings.suggested_fix: In child a3d7, extend the disabled-path test to invoke two or three existing CLI commands with the default config and assert zero-exit / no-web-import, covering the 'all existing commands behave unchanged' clause of the parent AC-3 oracle.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
